steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The sentence says traditional descriptions of pollination syndromes have led ecologists to question their usefulness when compared to "recent empirical observations of floral-trait combinations." The blank must name what exists between the two that creates that doubt.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to signal a mismatch between traditional descriptions and recent observations. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Proclamations of: bold public declarations of traditional descriptions — this suggests confidence, not a reason to question usefulness. ✗
  • Choice B — Affinities between: similarities or connections — if the two agreed, ecologists would have no reason for doubt. ✗
  • Choice C — Recurrences of: repeated appearances of traditional descriptions — repetition doesn't create doubt about usefulness. ✗
  • Choice D — Discrepancies between: disagreements or mismatches — a gap between traditional descriptions and recent observations is exactly what prompts ecologists to question the descriptions. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
